input.prompt: I have a .NET 10 project with a CSV of customer data (Age, Income, Region, PurchaseHistory columns). I want to predict whether a customer will churn (yes/no).

data.content: I have a .NET 10 project with a CSV of customer data (Age, Income, Region, PurchaseHistory columns). I want to predict whether a customer will churn (yes/no).
data.transformedContent:
<current_datetime>2026-04-05T01:35:10.721Z</current_datetime>

I have a .NET 10 project with a CSV of customer data (Age, Income, Region, PurchaseHistory columns). I want to predict whether a customer will churn (yes/no).

input.initialPrompt: I have a .NET 10 project with a CSV of customer data (Age, Income, Region, PurchaseHistory columns). I want to predict whether a customer will churn (yes/no).

data.reasoningText:
The user wants to build a churn prediction model using .NET 10 with ML.NET. They have a CSV with columns: Age, Income, Region, PurchaseHistory, and want to predict churn (yes/no binary classification).

Let me first explore what's already in the ChurnPredictor directory, then build out the solution.

data.reasoningText:
Now I have a clear picture. The project is a .NET 10 web app with a CSV file containing customer data. I need to:

1. Add ML.NET NuGet package
2. Create data models (CustomerData, ChurnPrediction)
3. Build an ML pipeline for binary classification
4. Add a prediction API endpoint
5. Train and expose the model

Let me build this out. The CSV has:
- Age (float)
- Income (float)
- Region (string - categorical)
- PurchaseHistory (float)
- Churned (string - "Yes"/"No" - this is the label)


I'll go with FastTree for the binary classification algorithm and implement the complete solution with the data models, training pipeline, and API endpoint.

toolCalls.name: edit

data.content: Now I need to make sure the CSV is copied to the output directory on build:

toolCalls.name: edit
toolCalls.args:
{"path": "/tmp/sv-f5da3a8040fb482a91e0f7da4639f045/ChurnPredictor/ChurnPredictor.csproj", "old_str": "  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.ML" Version="4.0.2" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>", "new_str": "  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.ML" Version="4.0.2" />
  </ItemGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <Content Include="Data\customers.csv" CopyToOutputDirectory="PreserveNewest" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>"}

data.content: Working perfectly. Let me stop the server:

Here's what was built:

**ML.NET binary classification** using SDCA Logistic Regression with your CSV data.

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `ChurnPredictor.csproj` | Added `Microsoft.ML 4.0.2` package + CSV copy rule |
| `Program.cs` | Full ML pipeline + REST API |

**Pipeline:** One-hot encodes `Region`, concatenates all features, normalizes, then trains SDCA logistic regression.

**API endpoints:**
- `GET /` — health check
- `POST /predict` — accepts `{age, income, region, purchaseHistory}`, returns `{willChurn, confidence}`

**Test results:**
- High-income, low-purchase customer → **will churn** (99.9% confidence)
- Young, frequent buyer → **won't churn** (0.05% confidence)

> **Note:** With only 5 training rows the model is illustrative. Add more data to `customers.csv` for production accuracy.
